skill.name: datafusion-query-advisor
skill.description: Reviews DataFusion SQL and DataFrame usage for performance issues such as pushdown, pruning, projection, joins, aggregation, streaming, and configuration.
skill.summary: Advises on DataFusion query plans, SQL patterns, and runtime settings.
skill.author: EmilLindfors

security_audit.summary: Static alerts for external commands, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance were dismissed as false positives after context review. The skill is markdown guidance for DataFusion query optimization, declares only Read and Grep tools, and shows no evidence of prompt injection, exfiltration, network access, or executable payloads.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Examples Misclassified
low_findings.description: The external command alerts point to inline backticks and fenced SQL or Rust examples in documentation. These examples are not executed by the skill, and the front matter only allows Read and Grep.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is markdown documentation and query examples, not runtime code. The skill has no scripts and declares only read-only analysis tools.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts are caused by ordinary text, including the skill description and SQL ORDER BY DESC syntax. No cryptographic API, hashing call, or encryption routine is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is YAML metadata, and line 373 is SQL ordering syntax. Neither location implements or recommends a weak cryptographic algorithm.

low_findings.title: False Positive: SQL Keywords Treated As Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts map to SQL JOIN, PARTITION BY, and sample user_id filters. They describe query optimization patterns and do not inspect the host system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are SQL query examples in a DataFusion performance guide. No commands gather system identity, files, processes, or network configuration.

content.value_statement: Slow DataFusion queries can waste compute and hide avoidable scan costs. This skill reviews SQL and DataFrame usage to suggest practical query and runtime optimizations.
actual_capabilities: Reviews DataFusion SQL for predicate pushdown, partition pruning, projection, joins, and aggregation issues.
Suggests safer DataFrame API patterns for filtering, selecting columns, streaming results, and avoiding large collects.
Explains how to use EXPLAIN output to verify filters, projections, partitions, and join plans.
Recommends SessionConfig and RuntimeConfig settings for parallelism, memory limits, and batch sizes.
Provides workload-specific guidance for analytics, point lookup, time-series, and join-heavy queries.
limitations: Does not execute benchmarks or measure runtime performance directly.
Cannot guarantee optimizer behavior without the exact DataFusion version and query plan.
Requires the user to provide representative SQL, DataFrame code, or EXPLAIN output.
Focuses on DataFusion and Parquet-style workloads, not general database tuning.

use_cases.title: Tune Rust Data Pipelines
use_cases.description: Review DataFusion code paths that process large Parquet datasets and identify avoidable scans or memory pressure.

use_cases.title: Improve Analytics Queries
use_cases.description: Check SQL for missing partition filters, broad projections, expensive aggregations, and join patterns before production use.

use_cases.title: Review Platform Defaults
use_cases.description: Assess DataFusion session settings for parallelism, batch size, streaming, and memory limits across shared workloads.

prompt_templates.title: Review A Slow Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this DataFusion SQL query for performance problems. Focus on filters, selected columns, joins, and partition pruning.

prompt_templates.title: Improve DataFrame Usage
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this DataFusion DataFrame code. Suggest changes that push filters earlier, reduce columns, and avoid collecting large results.

prompt_templates.title: Interpret An EXPLAIN Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this DataFusion EXPLAIN output. Identify whether filters, projections, partition pruning, and join choices are working as expected.

prompt_templates.title: Audit A Workload
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these DataFusion queries and runtime settings as one workload. Prioritize fixes by expected performance impact and operational risk.

output_examples.input: A query scans an events table for one day but filters only on a timestamp column.
output_examples.output: The review recommends adding year, month, and day filters so partition pruning can skip unrelated files. It also keeps the timestamp filter for correctness.

output_examples.input: A DataFrame pipeline collects all rows before applying application-side filtering.
output_examples.output: The review suggests applying filters and column selection in DataFusion before collection. It recommends streaming results when the output can be large.

output_examples.input: An EXPLAIN plan shows a table scan reading all columns from a wide Parquet table.
output_examples.output: The review highlights missing projection pushdown and suggests selecting only required columns to reduce I/O and memory usage.

best_practices: Share the SQL, DataFrame code, table layout, and data format when asking for a review.
Include EXPLAIN output before and after changes to confirm the optimizer applied the intended plan.
Prioritize partition pruning, predicate pushdown, and column projection before tuning smaller settings.
anti_patterns: Do not collect large result sets before filtering or projecting columns.
Do not rely on SELECT * for wide tables when only a few columns are needed.
Do not tune parallelism or batch size before checking query shape and data skipping.

faq.question: Does this skill run DataFusion queries?
faq.answer: No. It reviews provided queries, code, and plans, then suggests optimizations for the user to test.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: Provide SQL, DataFrame code, table partitioning details, file format, data size, and EXPLAIN output when available.

faq.question: Can it help with Parquet performance?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on predicate pushdown, projection, statistics, and partition pruning for Parquet-style workloads.

faq.question: Does it replace benchmarking?
faq.answer: No. It identifies likely improvements, but users should benchmark changes with representative data and workloads.

faq.question: Can it review DataFrame API code?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest earlier filters, narrower projections, streaming, and safer collection patterns.

faq.question: Is it specific to DataFusion?
faq.answer: Yes. Most guidance targets Apache DataFusion behavior and Rust data engineering workflows.
